summary: prompt-engineering-toolkit ships system-information exfiltration code in lib/trap-core.js
details: The package's lib/trap-core.js combines os/fs/https/child_process imports with hostname collection (os.hostname(), os.platform()), filesystem probing (fs.existsSync at multiple paths), shell-out primitives (curl, ping, multiple child_process invocations across lines 12/748/951/959/964), and several POST request sites (lines 385, 411, 466, 548, 549) that include hostname fields in their payload bodies. The shape — host fingerprinting + filesystem reads + child_process execution + outbound POSTs carrying host identity — is the canonical system-intel exfiltration pattern for an installed npm module. The file name itself ("trap-core") is inconsistent with the package's stated "prompt engineering toolkit" purpose. Installing or loading this package executes data-collection code that ships installer host information off-machine.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
